text:
To figure out how many times Sandra needs to babysit, we first need to determine the total number of eggs required to make 15 Spanish flans. Since each Spanish flan requires 3 eggs, we can calculate the total number of eggs needed as follows:

\[ \text{Total eggs needed} = 15 \text{ flans} \times 3 \text{ eggs/flan} = 45 \text{ eggs} \]

Next, we know that Sandra's neighbor gives her 9 eggs every time she babysits. To find out how many times Sandra needs to babysit to get 45 eggs, we divide the total number of eggs needed by the number of eggs she gets per babysitting session:

\[ \text{Number of babysitting sessions needed} = \frac{45 \text{ eggs}}{9 \text{ eggs/babysitting session}} = 5 \]

Therefore, Sandra needs to babysit 5 times to have enough eggs to make 15 Spanish flans for her school fundraiser.
